title: Peer Support & Mentoring
description: Peer mentors with lived experience walk alongside you on your recovery journey, helping with treatment navigation, transportation, food and ID applications, job searching, housing, and medical care — plus recovery, community, and emotional support. Harm-reduction based.
engage: Call 503-465-2749 or use the contact form at bridgestochange.com/contact to get connected with a peer mentor.
content:
What Peer Mentors Help With

Basic Needs

  • Treatment navigation
  • Transportation
  • Food assistance applications
  • Identification documents
  • Job searching
  • Housing resources
  • Medical care access

Recovery Support

  • Community building and peer connection
  • Stress management
  • Self-esteem development
  • Understanding from shared life experience

Approach

Bridges to Change believes participants are the experts in their own lives. Mentors use a harm-reduction approach and support each person in finding their own recovery path.

Who It's For

People in recovery from substance use, including justice-involved individuals and those reentering the community.

title: Supportive Housing
description: Multiple supportive housing models across Multnomah, Clackamas, Washington, and Wasco counties — including low-barrier stabilization housing (sobriety not required), corrections/reentry housing, mental health stabilization, treatment housing, culturally specific housing, and a 30–45 day Recovery Access Center in Gresham.
engage: Complete a housing referral form at bridgestochange.com or call 971-386-3338.
content:
Housing Programs

  • Stabilization Housing: Low-barrier housing for people new to recovery — sobriety is not a requirement to move in. Self-referral or community partner referral.
  • Corrections Housing: For individuals exiting incarceration; requires approval from a probation officer/department. Includes peer mentors and treatment.
  • Mental Health Stabilization: Safe, structured housing for individuals with severe and persistent mental health issues, in partnership with mental health courts.
  • Treatment Housing: For people engaged in substance use disorder treatment (Multnomah and Washington counties).
  • Recovery Access Center (RAC): A 30–45 day temporary program in Gresham with peer support, group meetings, two meals a day, laundry, job search, and ID help.
  • Culturally Specific Housing: Partners serving LGBTQ+, Black/African American, and Latine communities.

How to Access

Complete a housing referral form or call 971-386-3338.

title: Substance Use & Mental Health Treatment
description: Outpatient and intensive outpatient substance use treatment (in-person, Multnomah and Washington counties) with an appointment within 72 hours, plus telehealth mental health treatment open to anyone in the tri-county area, and co-occurring disorders treatment at the Foster site.
engage: Call 503-560-7800 or complete the new client form online to schedule an assessment.
content:
Treatment Options

  • Substance Use Disorder Treatment: In-person outpatient and intensive outpatient in Multnomah and Washington counties. Rapid access — an appointment within 72 hours. Led by Certified Alcohol and Drug Counselors running groups to build coping skills.
  • Mental Health Treatment: Telehealth, outpatient only, open to anyone in Multnomah, Washington, and Clackamas counties. Staffed by Qualified Mental Health Professionals.
  • Co-Occurring Disorders Treatment: Outpatient treatment for substance use and mental health together, at the Foster site in Multnomah County.

How to Access

Call or email to schedule your assessment and get connected with a peer mentor. Contact 503-560-7800 or complete a new client form online.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Immediate Care (Club Hope Drop-In)
description: Free, confidential, walk-in support open to anyone. Club Hope in Gresham offers peer mentoring, showers, laundry, food, rest areas, and recovery meetings. A Mobile Behavioral Health Team brings peer support, counseling, and supplies (including Narcan) to CareOregon members in the community.
engage: Walk into Club Hope at 100 SE Cleveland Ave, Gresham, Monday–Friday 8am–5pm (showers/laundry 10am–3pm). For the Mobile Behavioral Health Team, submit a referral online.
content:
Club Hope

  • Location: 100 SE Cleveland Ave, Gresham, OR 97080
  • Hours: Monday–Friday 8am–5pm (showers & laundry 10am–3pm)
  • Services: Peer mentoring, showers, laundry, food, rest areas, and recovery meetings throughout the week.

Mobile Behavioral Health Team (MBHT)

  • Available to CareOregon members
  • Brings services to individuals in their homes or communities
  • Peer mentors, professional counseling from Qualified Mental Health Professionals, and supplies including food, clothing, tents, and Narcan

Who It's For

Free, confidential, and open to anyone. Low-barrier — this is a walk-in support center, not a crisis or detox facility.

title: 24/7 Crisis Support Line
description: Free, confidential 24/7 crisis support for anyone experiencing domestic violence or sexual assault. Reach advocates by phone, chat, text, or email. Multilingual interpretation available in Arabic, Burmese, Chinese, Nepali, Russian, Somali, Spanish, and Vietnamese.
engage: Call the 24/7 crisis line at 1-888-235-5333 (local 503-235-5333), or email support@calltosafety.org. Chat and text support also available.
content:
Crisis Support

Call to Safety (formerly the Portland Women's Crisis Line) provides free, confidential, around-the-clock support, information, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault, and for their friends and family.

Ways to Reach Us

  • Crisis line: 1-888-235-5333 (24/7, free, confidential)
  • Local line: 503-235-5333
  • Email support: support@calltosafety.org
  • Chat & text support for those who prefer not to call

Language Access

Interpretation available in Arabic, Burmese, Chinese, Nepali, Russian, Somali, Spanish, and Vietnamese.

title: Emergency Domestic Violence Shelter
description: Safe, confidential emergency housing for people fleeing domestic violence, accessed through the 24/7 crisis line.
engage: Call the 24/7 crisis line at 1-888-235-5333 to be connected with emergency shelter.
content:
Emergency Shelter

Call to Safety connects survivors of domestic violence with safe emergency housing. Because shelter locations are confidential for safety, access is arranged through the 24/7 crisis line.

How to Access

Call 1-888-235-5333 (24/7, free, confidential) to speak with an advocate about emergency shelter options.

title: Support Groups
description: Peer support groups for survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault to connect with others who understand.
engage: Contact Call to Safety at 503-235-5333 or the crisis line at 1-888-235-5333 to learn about current support groups.
content:
Support Groups

Peer support gatherings where survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault can find community, understanding, and mutual support.

How to Join

Contact the organization to learn about current groups, schedules, and how to participate.

title: Safety Planning & Follow-Up Advocacy
description: Personalized safety planning (including technology/tech safety guidance) and ongoing follow-up advocacy for survivors after a crisis.
engage: Call the crisis line at 1-888-235-5333 to work with an advocate on a safety plan or ongoing advocacy.
content:
Safety Planning

Advocates help survivors create personalized safety strategies, including guidance on technology and digital safety.

Follow-Up Advocacy

Ongoing advocacy and support after the initial crisis, connecting survivors with continued assistance and resources.

How to Access

Reach an advocate through the 24/7 crisis line at 1-888-235-5333.

title: Bad Date Line
description: A confidential information-sharing line for sex workers to report and hear about dangerous or violent clients, supporting community safety.
engage: Call Call to Safety to access or contribute to the Bad Date Line. Crisis line: 1-888-235-5333.
content:
Bad Date Line

A safety-focused information-sharing service that lets sex workers report dangerous encounters and access warnings about unsafe clients. Confidential and non-judgmental.

How to Access

Contact Call to Safety by phone. All contact is confidential.

title: HIV + STI Testing
description: Free, confidential HIV and STI testing (HIV, syphilis, chlamydia, gonorrhea; some sites also hep C) with results in about 20 minutes. Aimed at gay/bi/queer men and trans folks, but no one is turned away. Walk-in and appointment options across Portland, Washington & Clackamas counties, Vancouver, and Longview.
engage: Book or find a walk-in site via capnw.org/get-tested, or call 503-223-5907. Home testing kits available for Washington State residents.
content:
What's Tested

HIV, syphilis, chlamydia, and gonorrhea at most locations; some sites also offer hepatitis C (HCV) screening.

Cost

All testing is confidential, free of charge, with results in about 20 minutes.

Locations & Access

  • Portland: Pivot @ Prism Health Belmont (by appointment); Testing 4 All (walk-in)
  • Washington County: Hillsboro clinic (walk-in & appointment)
  • Clackamas County: multiple sites
  • Vancouver, WA and Longview, WA (Longview walk-in only)

Who It's For

Aimed at gay, bisexual, and queer men and trans people — but everyone is welcome and no one is turned away.

title: PrEP & PEP (HIV Prevention)
description: Support accessing PrEP (pre-exposure prophylaxis) — pill or injectable medication that reduces HIV risk from sex by ~99% — plus PEP for post-exposure prevention. CAP Navigators handle insurance enrollment and patient-assistance programs for the uninsured, all free.
engage: Start at tinyurl.com/PrEP-CAP, email prep@capnw.org, or call 503-223-5907.
content:
PrEP

Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis is preventive medication (pill or injectable). Taken as prescribed, it reduces the risk of getting HIV from sex by about 99% and from injection drug use by at least 74%.

Who It's For

People not living with HIV at higher risk — those with an HIV-positive partner, inconsistent condom use, involvement in sex work, a recent STI, or who share needles.

Cost & Insurance

Oregon Health Plan and private insurance cover PrEP fully, including medication, office visits, and labs. For the uninsured, patient assistance programs cover medication costs. CAP Navigators help with enrollment.

PEP

Post-Exposure Prophylaxis is also available with education, counseling, and follow-up support.

Free Services

Education, counseling, peer support, provider referrals, and insurance enrollment assistance.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Housing Services
description: Supportive housing and rental assistance prioritizing people living with HIV and serving the LGBTQ+ community — long-term supportive housing (waitlist open), housing readiness (drop-in, no appointment), housing navigation, emergency rental assistance, and shelter referrals.
engage: Drop in for Housing Readiness (Mon 12–3pm, Tue 9am–12pm, Thu 11am–1:30pm) or email rentassist@capnw.org / call 503-517-3590.
content:
Services

  • Supportive Housing Program (SHP): Long-term housing for people experiencing or at risk of homelessness. Waitlist is open and accepting applicants.
  • Housing Readiness: Help with applications, fair housing education, and tenant rights. No appointment needed — just drop in.
  • Housing Navigation: Support with searches, applications, ESA letters, and eviction proceedings.
  • Emergency Rental Assistance: Application fees, first month's rent, security deposits, utility assistance, and mortgage help.
  • Shelter Referrals: Connections to transitional shelters through partner organizations.

Eligibility

Prioritizes qualified people living with HIV; serves LGBTQ+ communities broadly. In Washington, clients must be enrolled in Medical Case Management and referred by their case manager.

title: Esther's Pantry (Food Assistance)
description: A choice-model food bank serving over 500 clients a month with supplemental food and personal care items, in Milwaukie. Clients select items from stocked shelves. Income-qualified (below 300% of federal poverty guidelines).
engage: Visit Esther's Pantry at 10202 SE 32nd Ave, Suites 601 & 502, Milwaukie during open hours (Mon 1–4pm, Tue 2–5pm, Wed 1–1:30pm, Thu 2–5pm).
content:
What It Provides

Esther's Pantry is a food bank where clients select food from well-stocked shelves to help support and maintain good health, plus personal care items. It serves over 500 clients each month.

Eligibility

Monthly household income below $3,765 for one person (300% of federal poverty guidelines), plus an additional $1,345 allowed per household member.

Location & Hours

10202 SE 32nd Ave, Suites 601 & 502, Milwaukie, OR 97222.

  • Monday: 1:00–4:00 PM
  • Tuesday: 2:00–5:00 PM
  • Wednesday: 1:00–1:30 PM
  • Thursday: 2:00–5:00 PM

title: Support Services & Case Management
description: Peer support from certified specialists living with HIV, short-term CareLink case management to connect newly diagnosed or out-of-care clients to HIV medical care, and drop-in Service Centers for referrals, bus fare, snacks, and computer access.
engage: Walk into a Service Center (no appointment needed), email carelink@capnw.org for CareLink, or housingandsupportcontact@cascadeaids.org for peer support.
content:
Programs

  • Peer Support Services: Certified specialists build relationships based on shared experience of living with HIV, mental health, and/or substance use challenges. For clients living with HIV, at or below 300% FPL, who want mental health or substance use support.
  • CareLink: Short-term case management to connect you to HIV medical providers, schedule and attend appointments, discuss medication adherence, and make referrals. For people newly diagnosed, out of HIV care over 6 months, or currently/recently incarcerated.
  • Service Centers: One-stop shop for referrals, bus fare, snacks, and computer access.

How to Access

Service Centers welcome walk-ins, no appointment necessary. CareLink accepts self-referrals at carelink@capnw.org.

title: Behavioral Health Counseling
description: Mental and behavioral health counseling for LGBTQ+ people and anyone seeking affirming care, provided through Prism Health, plus embedded Cascadia counseling for CAP housing participants.
engage: Access counseling through Prism Health (prismhealth.org/services/mental-health), or if you're in a CAP housing program, ask CAP staff for an internal referral. Call 503-223-5907 for info.
content:
Services

  • General mental and behavioral health counseling
  • Mental health specialist referrals
  • Affirming care tailored for LGBTQ+ individuals

Who It's For

Available to anyone regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity. CAP housing participants (or those on the housing waitlist) can access counseling through a Cascadia mental health provider embedded at CAP's Davis Street office.

How to Access

Through Prism Health, or via internal referral for CAP housing participants.
modes: In Person; Online; By Appointment Only

title: Medical Case Management (HIV)
description: Comprehensive case management for people living with HIV in Southwest Washington — linkage to medical care, medication adherence, insurance navigation, benefits help, and referrals for transportation, housing, food, dental, mental health, and substance use support.
engage: Call the intake line: English 360-200-8712, Spanish 360-218-4117. Offices in Vancouver and Longview, WA.
content:
What It Includes

  • Linkage to medical care and treatment
  • Medication adherence support
  • Insurance navigation
  • Benefits application and eligibility assistance
  • Health and wellness goal planning
  • Referrals for transportation, housing, food, dental care, mental health, substance use treatment, and psychosocial support

Eligibility

Anyone living with HIV in Southwest Washington (Clark, Skamania, Cowlitz, and Wahkiakum counties).

How to Access

Call the intake line (English 360-200-8712, Spanish 360-218-4117). A brief phone assessment is followed by a full intake with a Medical Case Manager. Offices in Vancouver and Longview.

title: Health Insurance Navigation
description: Free help from CAP's Health Insurance Coordinators with applications, coverage navigation, and enrollment — Oregon Health Plan, private plans, and the Federal Marketplace. Open to everyone regardless of HIV status or income.
content:
What It Includes

CAP's Health Insurance Coordinators guide you through insurance applications, coverage navigation, and enrollment across multiple plan types — Oregon Health Plan, private insurance, and Federal Marketplace plans. They don't enroll Medicare directly but can help you use Medicare coverage.

Who It's For

Everyone, at no cost, regardless of HIV status or income level.

How to Access

Email insuranceassistance@cascadeaids.org or call the Portland office at 503-223-5907.

title: Primary Care (Prism Health)
description: Affirming, high-quality primary care through Prism Health at its Belmont location — day-to-day healthcare and coordination of specialist referrals, welcoming to all identities including patients with complex needs.
engage: Visit prismhealth.org or contact CAP's main office at 503-223-5907 / info@capnw.org.
content:
What's Offered

Comprehensive primary care — your first point of contact for day-to-day healthcare, with coordination of specialist referrals. Delivered through Prism Health.

Who It's For

All individuals regardless of identity, including those with complex medical needs. A welcoming, affirming environment.

Location & Access

Prism Health, Belmont location. Visit prismhealth.org or contact CAP's main office at 503-223-5907.
